requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must meet material participation and time requirements to qualify as a real estate professional, allowing passive rental losses to offset active income
criteria: More than 50% of personal services performed during the year must be in real property trades or businesses
Must perform more than 750 hours of services in real property trades or businesses during the tax year
Real property trades or businesses include development, redevelopment, construction, reconstruction, acquisition, conversion, rental, operation, management, leasing, or brokerage
Must materially participate in each rental real estate activity to deduct losses against non-passive income
Maintain contemporaneous time logs and detailed records of all real estate activities and hours
Cannot be an employee in a real property business unless you own 5% or more of the business
Spouse can qualify if either spouse separately meets both requirements and files joint return
Must elect to aggregate all rental real estate activities to meet material participation tests

real_estate_professional_requirements.hours_requirement: Must exceed 750 hours in real property trades or businesses annually
real_estate_professional_requirements.percentage_requirement: More than 50% of total personal services must be in real property activities
real_estate_professional_requirements.material_participation: Must materially participate in rental activities under IRC §469(c)(7) tests
real_estate_professional_requirements.aggregation_election: Can elect to treat all rental real estate as single activity for material participation
real_estate_professional_requirements.documentation_critical: Contemporaneous records essential for IRS audit defense and qualification proof
real_estate_professional_requirements.employee_restriction: W-2 employees in real estate generally cannot qualify unless 5%+ owner

restrictions: Status must be met each tax year; prior year qualification does not carry forward automatically
Cannot qualify if employed full-time in non-real estate trade or business exceeding 50% of services
Passive activity loss limitations apply to rental activities where material participation not established
Time spent as investor (reviewing financials, arranging financing) generally does not count toward 750 hours
Aggregation election is binding for current and future years unless material change in circumstances
Losses may still be limited by at-risk rules and basis limitations even if qualifying as real estate professional
State tax treatment may differ from federal qualification creating additional compliance complexity
Short-term rental activities (average stay 7 days or less) subject to different rules and may not qualify
